title: NFL insider would be ‘surprised’ if Patriots don’t land A.J. Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NFL insider Ian Rapoport speculates Patriots will likely acquire A.J. Brown despite no recent talks between the teams.
Brown's dead cap hit changes after June 1, affecting trade dynamics; Eagles seek a 2027 first-round pick.
There's speculation Patriots receiver Kayshon Boutte could be part of a trade deal for Brown.

title: Here’s how much a last-minute trip to Cape Cod for Memorial Day weekend costs
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A day trip to Cape Cod costs approximately $162.75, including gas, beach parking, food, and souvenirs.
A couple's weekend getaway totals about $1,867.55, covering lodging, meals, and entertainment expenses.
A family of four vacation costs an estimated $2,331.35, incorporating hotel stay, meals, and activities.

title: 5 arrested in brawl at Dorchester courthouse
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Five individuals were arrested following a brawl at Dorchester Division of the Boston Municipal Court around 10 a.m. Wednesday.
The suspects include four adults aged 19-20 and a 17-year-old; their identities include Syncere Johnson and Shamar Passley-Clark.
All face charges of affray and disrupting court proceedings, with additional charges for the 17-year-old, including assault on a police officer.

title: Mass. man pleads guilty to prostitution and fraud charges over strip club activities, COVID loan
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Kenneth Denning, 69, from Holland, Massachusetts, pleaded guilty to prostitution and fraud charges related to his strip club in Connecticut.
Denning defrauded the IRS of nearly $3 million in taxable business income and obtained a $149,900 federal COVID-19 relief loan fraudulently.
Denning agreed to pay $550,000 in restitution to the IRS and $150,000 to the Small Business Administration as part of his plea deal.

title: The Red Sox have a clear formula for success. Is it sustainable?
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox have lowest batting average since 1968 and lowest ERA since 1992, currently hitting .240 with a 3.68 ERA.
Red Sox are 14-6 when scoring four or more runs, but only 8-21 when scoring three or fewer.
Despite strong pitching, consistent offensive output is crucial for playoff chances, compared to past playoff teams and Red Sox history.

title: Alleged Memorial Drive shooter found dangerous, ordered held without bail
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Tyler Brown, charged with shooting on Memorial Drive, was found dangerous and held without bail after a hearing.
Brown faces multiple felony charges, including assault to murder, after allegedly injuring two people in the Cambridge attack.
The shooter with a history of criminal and mental health issues appeared virtually in court while recovering from gunshot wounds.

title: Isaiah Thomas hopes ‘second act’ with Celtics will help him become a GM
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Isaiah Thomas has joined the Boston Celtics as a scout, aiming for a future executive role.
Thomas continues to base himself in Seattle, assisting with the Celtics' scouting on the West Coast.
Thomas played for nine teams before retiring with the Suns in 2024 and now pursues a career in management.

title: Woman pleads not guilty in teen’s 2025 drowning in N.H.’s Lake Winnipesaukee
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Buffy Ann Doyle, 50, of Nashua, pleaded not guilty to charges related to a teen's drowning in Lake Winnipesaukee.
Doyle faces charges of felony negligent homicide, witness tampering, and falsifying evidence for the June 2025 incident.
The 17-year-old victim drowned while trying to retrieve a bag after allegedly being provided alcohol by Doyle.

title: Cumberland Farms makes a splash with ‘$99’ coffee
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A Cumberland Farms ad mistakenly listed coffee at $99, causing an online stir regarding inflation.
The ad featured errors like steaming iced coffee and odd image perspectives, sparking AI creation speculation.
Cumberland Farms, with over 1,500 US locations, has not clarified if the 99-cent coffee error was AI-related.

title: Boston City Council votes to keep recent pay raises
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council members' salaries increased from $103,500 to $125,000 from 2024 to 2026.
Councilors Erin Murphy and Ed Flynn proposed rescinding the raises, arguing the funds should aid residents amid budget cuts.
The proposal was rejected; Councilor Sharon Durkan argued maintaining salaries helps council members afford living expenses in Boston.
